channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Krystal Mejes and Heath Jornales reminisce their meaningful moments inside ‘PBB Collab 2.0’
item.description: In a heartfelt feature from ABS-CBN, former Pinoy Big Brother Collab 2.0 housemates Krystal Mejes and Heath Jornales looked back on their most unforgettable experiences during their stay inside the ho...

item.title: RSS Clarifies Stance on Caste Census, Emphasising Social Unity Over Political Division
item.description: In an interview with The Times of India, RSS national publicity head Sunil Ambekar articulated the organisation’s nuanced position on the caste census. Ambekar stated that the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sa...

item.title: Aviation Ministry Warns of Possible Increase in Airfares and Fuel Prices Due to Rising Global Tensions
item.description: According to India’s Civil Aviation Minister, the ongoing geopolitical tensions between Israel and Iran could have a direct impact on global oil prices, leading to a likely increase in aviation turbin...

item.title: Stephanie Ruhle Recalls Surprising Phone Call With President Trump Amid Iran War
item.description: MS NOW host Stephanie Ruhle shared the unusual story of receiving a surprise phone call from President Donald Trump while on her way to the airport. After weeks of trying to reach him amid the escalat...

item.title: UCF’s John Bol accidentally scores on own basket in NCAA Tournament loss to UCLA
item.description: During Friday night’s first-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament, the University of Central Florida (UCF) suffered a tough 75–71 loss to UCLA, partly due to an unusual mishap by center John Bol. ...

item.title: Mandela’s Sharpeville Visit in 1996 Marked a Symbol of Hope for South Africa
item.description: In May 1996, South Africa adopted its democratic constitution after years of struggle. Later that year, on December 10, President Nelson Mandela signed it into law not in a grand hall, but in Sharpevi...

item.title: Analysts say Melbourne’s housing market offers long-term potential despite economic and tax challenges
item.description: Analysts suggest that Melbourne’s property market continues to hold strong long-term potential for investors, even though short-term conditions remain less favourable. Despite Victoria’s position as o...

item.title: Police edge closer to solving 1976 disappearance of Renee and Andrew MacRae after killer’s death
item.description: Nearly fifty years after the disappearance of Highland mother Renee MacRae and her three-year-old son Andrew, police in Scotland may be close to uncovering their remains. The pair vanished on a foggy ...

item.title: Pentagon cuts ties with Anthropic over ethical concerns in AI drone warfare
item.description: The article discusses the recent fallout between Anthropic, a leading artificial intelligence company, and the Pentagon after the AI firm refused to provide unrestricted access to its technology for m...

item.title: Police Sergeant Sentenced for Manslaughter in Iringa
item.description: Iringa High Court has sentenced Police Sergeant Rogers Mmari to two years in prison for manslaughter following an incident on December 14, 2024. The court found that Sergeant Mmari, stationed at Ipogo...

item.title: Senate probes runway excursion at Wilson Airport after Kisumu flight incident
item.description: A passenger plane carrying 39 people veered off the runway at Wilson Airport in Nairobi on Friday evening, March 20, 2026, prompting the Senate to demand answers on safety. The flight had come from Ki...

item.title: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Chief and Special Counsel, Passes Away at 81
item.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director who led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 US presidential election, has passed away at the age of 81. Mueller, who was appointed as a spec...

item.title: Clashes in Mathura after cow vigilante dies in road accident amidst smuggling allegations
item.description: Tensions flared in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, on Saturday after the death of Chandrashekhar, a self-styled cow vigilante known locally as 'Farsa Wale Baba'. He was fatally struck by a truck in the Kosi K...

item.title: Long-term effects of bacterial meningitis extend beyond hospital treatment, study finds
item.description: Bacterial meningitis is widely known as a life-threatening illness, with the World Health Organization estimating that roughly one in six infected individuals die even with prompt medical care. Howeve...

item.title: Negros Occidental radio blocktimer killed in front of his home
item.description: A community radio blocktimer identified as Julio Calo from 88.3 DNN News FM was shot and killed by a lone gunman in La Castellana, Negros Occidental on Friday. According to police reports, Calo was at...

item.title: Filipino Muslims mark Eid’l Fitr across various regions
item.description: The Muslim community in the Philippines celebrated Eid’l Fitr on March 21, 2026, marking the end of the sacred Ramadan fasting period. The holiday was officially observed on a Saturday, despite Presid...

item.title: Labor achieves decisive victory in South Australian election amid One Nation surge
item.description: In the South Australian state election held on 21 March 2026, Labor secured a commanding win, projected to take 31 of the 47 lower house seats, already a majority. When marginal seats are considered, ...

item.title: Teenage star Luan Giliomee inspires Sharks’ big URC win over Munster as Mapimpi reaches 100th match milestone
item.description: Teenager Luan Giliomee made an unforgettable United Rugby Championship debut as the Sharks crushed Munster 45–0 at Kings Park. The 19-year-old fullback, fresh from the Sevens circuit, scored the team’...

item.title: Asake Drops New Single 'Worship' with DJ Snake During Eid Festivities
item.description: Asake don make 2026 extra special as e release him new single wey him call 'Worship', featuring French-Algerian superstar DJ Snake. The song come out on Friday, March 20, which na the day wey Muslims ...

item.title: Meningitis Cases in Kent Reach 34 Amid Vaccination Efforts
item.description: The number of meningitis cases in Kent has risen to 34, according to the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA), up from 29 reported earlier in the week. The outbreak has tragically claimed the lives of a ...
